name: Logistic Regression
applicable_scenarios: model a binary outcome as a function of predictors
interpretable classification baseline with odds-ratio coefficients
python_template:
import numpy as np
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

def fit_logistic(X, y, C=1.0):
    model = LogisticRegression(C=C, max_iter=1000, solver='lbfgs')
    model.fit(X, y)
    return model

def predict_proba(model, X):
    return model.predict_proba(X)[:, 1]
common_pitfalls: perfect / quasi-separation yields infinite coefficients; regularize
imbalanced classes distort thresholding — tune threshold or use class_weight
probabilities need calibration (Platt / isotonic) for decision-theoretic use
